Cycle 1 fix: home guard now uses file-based transport (proven to survive -n auto), not worker stdout. Detection is non-vacuous. ruff+mypy clean.

review.reference: Re-review passed (paula-patterns) — cycle-2 rejection (stdout/print transport that xdist drops = permanent no-op) is RESOLVED via file transport. Independently verified: my own -n 2 --dist loadfile probe wrote Path.home() to a pid-named file and the parent read it back non-empty (/Users/robert). Defect gone and pinned by test_probe_body_uses_file_transport_not_stdout (asserts no print). Empty transport is now a HARD FAILURE (_NO_WORKER_HOMES / assert worker_homes), DISTINCT from the pre-WP04 clean skip (_SKIP_PRE_WP04), so a transport breakage cannot silently disarm SC-006. Non-vacuity proven via the real T008 assert_mutation_caught on _sentinel_unchanged (rewrite + None->present). pragma:no-cover only on the WP04-gated subprocess body; decision logic is pure-unit tested. Suite: 31 passed/1 skipped; guard 100% cov, helpers 93-100%; ruff+mypy clean. (--force: base-ref edge case, commits 3e27cf651+fa0887aa4 verified present.)
